title: Layouts for Printing

Layouts for Printing

Layouts for Printing

The following examples illustrate the effect that the component layout has on the printed output.

Example: Printing a Single Entity

If an entity is drawn with only one occurrence, labels are repeated for each instance of entity expansion.

This layout produces the following output. Notice that the detached labels have been expanded to accommodate the print font.

Label outside
 frames: Start
  Detached label
  inside entity
 Abbreviation BW
 Full name Bruce Wayne
 Address Wayne Mansion
 City Gotham City
  Detached label
  inside entity
 Abbreviation CK
 Full name Clark Kent
 Address
 City Smallville
  Detached label
  inside entity
 Abbreviation DD
 Full name Donald Duck
 Address 4 Quack Lane
 City Duckville
  Label outside
  frames: End

Example: Printing Multiple Occurrences

If the entity is drawn with multiple occurrences, labels are printed for the first occurrence but are not repeated in subsequent occurrences. In the following layout, the detached labels have been enlarged to improve the print layout and two occurrences have been drawn.

This form layout produces the following output. In the layout, the complete height of the entity is 6 lines. This is reflected in the output, even though the labels are not repeated.

Label outside frames: Start
 Detached label inside entity
 Abbreviation BW
 Full name Bruce Wayne
 Address Wayne Mansion
 City Gotham City
 CK
 Clark Kent
 Smallville
 DD
 Donald Duck
 4 Quack Lane
 Duckville
 Label outside frames: End

Example: Column Layouts in Reports

For printed output in columns you can make use of Uniface's behavior when printing multiple occurrences. The following layout features nested entities, with the inner entity drawn with multiple occurrences. The inner entity labels are placed in the outer entity so that the inner entity height is only one line. A header frame and trailer frame have also been drawn.

This layout produces the following output. Notice the following:

  • Labels preceding the inner entity are not repeated if printing continues to another page. To prevent this type of inconvenient page break, click the inner entity frame and choose Printing >Print Frame on Same Page.
  • The labels for the inner entity are printed even when there is no associated data. To prevent this, you can enclose the labels and the inner entity in a named area frame and suppress printing. Right-click the names entity frame and choose Printing >Suppress Print if Empty. 
------------------------------------------------------------------
|Report Header  |
------------------------------------------------------------------
 Outer entity: DEPARTMENT
 Abbreviation DEV
 Department Development
 Manager Bruce Wayne
 Inner entity: PERSON
 Abbreviation Full Name
 AA Alpa
 BB Bruce Banner
 BD Buck Danny
 GF Guust Flater
 MP Miss Parker
 Outer entity: DEPARTMENT
 Abbreviation MAN
 Department Management
 Manager Guust Flater
 Inner entity: PERSON
 Abbreviation Full Name
 BW Bruce Wayne
 CK Clark Kent
 JD John Doe
 JR Jack Rabe
 LL Lois Lane
 PP Peter Parker
<snip>
------------------------------------------------------------------
|Report trailer  |
------------------------------------------------------------------
------------------------------------------------------------------
|Report Header  |
------------------------------------------------------------------
 MW Mary-Jane Watson
 Outer entity: DEPARTMENT
 Abbreviation FCL
 Department Facilities
 Manager Bruce Banner
 Inner entity: PERSON
 Abbreviation Full Name
 Outer entity: DEPARTMENT
 Abbreviation TST
 Department Testing
 Manager Fleur de Boer
 Inner entity: PERSON
 Abbreviation Full Name
 DD Donald Duck
 FB Fleur de Boer
 JE Jack Daniels
<snip>
------------------------------------------------------------------
|Report trailer  |
------------------------------------------------------------------
Related Links
  • Area Frames for Printing

    In reports, area frames can be used to add and protect text as part of an entity in Report components.
